title: Add helm-validate CI job with lint and template tests
summary: Post-external-review improvement cycle addressing 4 warnings and 1 info item from reviewer berendt. Changes: (1) added inline comment explaining --verify=false on helm-unittest plugin install, (2) split docs DAG section from conditional jobs to its own heading, (3) created certificate_test.yaml with 5 test cases covering Issuer/Certificate enabled/disabled states, DNS names, and issuer reference, (4) updated review pattern files and progress tracking. All 7 helm-unittest suites (45 tests) pass. Helm lint and all 4 template scenarios exit 0. CI YAML is syntactically valid.

tests_checklist.item: All helm-unittest suites pass (7 suites, 45 tests)
certificate_test.yaml covers enabled state (Issuer + Certificate assertions)
certificate_test.yaml verifies DNS names match template output

code_quality_checklist.item: certificate_test.yaml follows existing test file pattern (comment header, suite, templates, documentIndex, set for disabled)
CI YAML change is minimal (single comment addition)
Review pattern files follow established format (name, review-area, detection-hint, severity, occurrences, sections)
Documentation entry for certificate_test.yaml added to ci-workflow.md table

security_checklist.item: --verify=false rationale documented with inline comment (W-002 fix)
Review pattern for TLS/signature verification updated with CC-0041 occurrence

architecture_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and targeted to external review feedback only
No scope creep beyond addressing the 4 warnings and 1 info item

dry_yagni_checklist.item: certificate_test.yaml reuses same patterns as webhook_test.yaml

fail_fast_checklist.item: Tests assert both positive (enabled) and negative (disabled) cases

defensive_checklist.item: certificate_test.yaml verifies both documents (Issuer at index 0, Certificate at index 1)

suggested_improvements: docs/reference/ci-workflow.md:84 — The DAG section now labels docs as 'Independent Jobs (always run, no dependencies)' but the actual CI YAML shows docs has needs: [changes] and if: needs.changes.outputs.docs == 'true' (lines 270-271). This pre-existing inaccuracy was restructured per the external reviewer's W-001 suggestion. Consider correcting the DAG to show docs under 'Conditional Jobs' with its actual dependency: 'docs ──> needs: [changes], if: docs == true'. This would match the actual workflow behavior.
next_steps: Verify helm-unittest v1.0.3 is the latest release (W-003 from external review — non-blocking)
